title: angelonia pubescens
intro: Angelonia pubescens, also known as the Angel's Trumpet, is a perennial plant native to Central and South America, characterized by its clusters of showy, trumpet-shaped flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties. The plant's leaves and flowers are rich in flavonoids, alkaloids, and other compounds that provide several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, Angelonia pubescens is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to leverage its medicinal properties, although caution is advised due to its potential toxicity when ingested in large quantities.
